_comment: Quiz data. 'answer' is the 0-based index of the correct option. Each question is scored equally; total = correct / N * 100.

quizzes.title: Investing Fundamentals
quizzes.blurb: The vocabulary — what investing, stocks, mutual funds, budgets and goals actually are.

questions.q: What is investing?
options: Putting money into assets that may grow in value

questions.q: Which of the following is an investment?
What does a stock represent?
What is a mutual fund?
options: A collection of investments managed by professionals

questions.q: Ravi invests ₹10,000. After a year, it grows to ₹11,000. What was the main reason Ravi invested?
options: To grow his money over time

questions.q: What is a financial goal?
options: A target you want to achieve with money

questions.q: What is a budget?
Aryan wants to save ₹1 lakh for college in 4 years. This ₹1 lakh target is his:
Which investment is generally considered safer?
Riya wants money for a vacation next year and retirement in 30 years. Why is it important to identify goals before investing?

quizzes.title: Risk, Diversification & Inflation
quizzes.blurb: Why prices rise, why spreading bets lowers risk, and what insurance is really for.

questions.q: What is investment risk?
What is diversification?
Which portfolio is more diversified?
What is inflation?
If inflation is high, your money's purchasing power:
Which investment usually carries the highest risk?
Why is diversification useful?
Which investor is generally willing to take more risk?
What is the main purpose of insurance?
What happens if all investments perform poorly?

quizzes.title: Scenario Challenges
quizzes.blurb: Real situations — read the scenario, then pick the most accurate call.

questions.scenario: Aarav has ₹20,000 to invest. Option A: invest all ₹20,000 in one technology company. Option B: invest ₹5,000 each in four different companies from different industries.
questions.q: Which statement is most accurate?
options: Option A is more diversified than Option B
Option B is likely to have lower risk due to diversification
Both options have exactly the same risk

questions.scenario: Priya keeps ₹1,00,000 in cash for 10 years. During that period, prices of goods and services rise significantly.
questions.q: What is the most likely outcome?
options: Her money automatically grows with inflation

questions.scenario: Rahul is choosing between Investment X (expected return 5% per year, low risk) and Investment Y (expected return 15% per year, high risk).
questions.q: Which statement is generally true?
options: Higher potential returns are often associated with higher risk
Higher returns always come with lower risk
Low-risk investments always outperform high-risk investments
Risk has no relation to returns

questions.scenario: Ananya has ₹50,000 saved and plans to use it for emergency medical expenses if needed.
questions.q: Which option is most suitable?
options: Keep it in a readily accessible account
Invest all of it in a highly volatile stock
Invest all of it in cryptocurrency

questions.scenario: Rohan wants to buy a car in 6 years. He starts investing regularly toward this goal.
questions.q: What is the main advantage of defining a financial goal before investing?
options: It helps choose investments that match the goal and time horizon
It removes the need for a budget

questions.scenario: Neha owns shares in 10 different companies. One of those companies performs poorly and its stock price falls sharply.
questions.q: Why might diversification help in this situation?
options: The loss in one investment may be partly offset by others
The stock price cannot fall further

questions.scenario: Kabir wants exposure to many companies but has only ₹2,000 to invest.
questions.q: Why might a mutual fund be a suitable option?
options: It allows instant diversification through a single investment

questions.scenario: A student notices that at the end of every month they have no savings despite receiving a fixed allowance.
questions.q: What is the best first step?
options: Create a budget to track income and expenses

questions.scenario: Two investors each invest ₹10,000. Investor A withdraws after 1 year. Investor B leaves the investment untouched for 15 years (assume positive average growth).
questions.q: Who is more likely to benefit from compounding?

questions.scenario: An investor wants moderate growth but is uncomfortable with large fluctuations in value.
questions.q: Which choice is generally more suitable?
options: Investing everything in one small startup
A diversified portfolio of different asset types
Investing based on social media tips

questions.scenario: Two investors each have ₹1,00,000. Investor A puts all the money into one stock. Investor B invests in a diversified portfolio. The stock market falls by 20%, but one company collapses completely.
questions.q: Who is likely to suffer the larger loss?
